query_normalized: evaluating the impact of data scaling on statistical test sensitivity statistics
rationale: This paper addresses the user's question by providing an empirical and theoretical analysis of how test statistics (specifically sums of rank correlations) behave and achieve power (sensitivity) under high-dimensional scaling regimes where the number of variables $m$ scales with or exceeds the sample size $n$. This satisfies acceptance criteria (a) and (b) as it measures the mechanism of statistical test sensitivity in the context of data scaling (high dimensions) and establishes the power prope
